pure OTel .NET SDKs in ASP.NET Core, using the OtlpExporter forwarding to the native collector that then forwards the signals to various targets.\\n\\nAs I now need to send to Azure Monitor and don\\u0026#39;t want to use the community collector that should support Azure Monitor, I\\u0026#39;m turning to using `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`.\\n\\nThe docs lists usage that looks more or less like this:\\n\\n```csharp\\nservices.AddOpenTelemetry().UseAzureMonitorExporter(opts =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    // Configure    \\n});\\n```\\n\\nThe solution I already have in place is controlled via existing config allowing for just adding the signals you want and it also uses OTel samplers etc. and only the instrumentation and metrics of interest.\\n\\n**Can I ignore doing the call to** `UseAzureMonitorExporter` and instead use your extension methods for each respective signals and use native samplers etc. or will I risk missing **important** features/tweaks from the Azure NuGet e.g. getting duplicated data or similar due to e.g. Azure SDKs using custom Http instrumentation or similar?\\n\\n**Simplified example Tracing and Metrics:**\\n\\n```csharp\\ntelemetryBuilder.WithMetrics(metrics =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    metrics\\n        .SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder)\\n        .AddMeter(meters);\\n\\n    metrics.AddAzureMonitorMetricExporter(mo =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        mo.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        mo.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        mo.Credential = credential;\\n        mo.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n\\ntelemetryBuilder.WithTracing(tracing =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    tracing\\n        .SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder)\\n        .AddSource(sources);\\n    \\n    var ratioSampler = new TraceIdRatioBasedSampler(samplingRatio);                \\n    tracing.SetSampler(new ParentBasedSampler(ratioSampler));\\n\\n    tracing\\n        .AddAspNetCoreInstrumentation()\\n        .AddHttpClientInstrumentation();\\n\\n    tracing.AddAzureMonitorTraceExporter(to =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        to.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        to.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        to.Credential = credential;\\n        to.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n\\n**Simplified example Logs:**\\n\\n```csharp\\nlogging.AddOpenTelemetry(lo =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    lo.SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder);\\n\\n    lo.AddAzureMonitorLogExporter(alo =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        alo.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        alo.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        alo.Credential = credential;\\n        alo.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n\\n### OpenTelemtry without UseAzureMonitorExporter\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0\\n\\n### Query/Question\\n\\nI currently have a solution using pure OTel .NET SDKs in ASP.NET Core, using the OtlpExporter forwarding to the native collector that then forwards the signals to various targets.\\n\\nAs I now need to send to Azure Monitor and don\\u0026#39;t want to use the community collector that should support Azure Monitor, I\\u0026#39;m turning to using `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`.\\n\\nThe docs lists usage that looks more or less like this:\\n\\n```csharp\\nservices.AddOpenTelemetry().UseAzureMonitorExporter(opts =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    // Configure    \\n});\\n```\\n\\nThe solution I already have in place is controlled via existing config allowing for just adding the signals you want and it also uses OTel samplers etc. and only the instrumentation and metrics of interest.\\n\\n**Can I ignore doing the call to** `UseAzureMonitorExporter` and instead use your extension methods for each respective signals and use native samplers etc. or will I risk missing **important** features/tweaks from the Azure NuGet e.g. getting duplicated data or similar due to e.g. Azure SDKs using custom Http instrumentation or similar?\\n\\n**Simplified example Tracing and Metrics:**\\n\\n```csharp\\ntelemetryBuilder.WithMetrics(metrics =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    metrics\\n        .SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder)\\n        .AddMeter(meters);\\n\\n    metrics.AddAzureMonitorMetricExporter(mo =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        mo.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        mo.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        mo.Credential = credential;\\n        mo.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n\\ntelemetryBuilder.WithTracing(tracing =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    tracing\\n        .SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder)\\n        .AddSource(sources);\\n    \\n    var ratioSampler = new TraceIdRatioBasedSampler(samplingRatio);                \\n    tracing.SetSampler(new ParentBasedSampler(ratioSampler));\\n\\n    tracing\\n        .AddAspNetCoreInstrumentation()\\n        .AddHttpClientInstrumentation();\\n\\n    tracing.AddAzureMonitorTraceExporter(to =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        to.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        to.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        to.Credential = credential;\\n        to.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n\\n**Simplified example Logs:**\\n\\n```csharp\\nlogging.AddOpenTelemetry(lo =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    lo.SetResourceBuilder(resourceBuilder);\\n\\n    lo.AddAzureMonitorLogExporter(alo =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        alo.EnableLiveMetrics = false;\\n        alo.DisableOfflineStorage = true;\\n        alo.Credential = credential;\\n        alo.ConnectionString = connectionString;\\n    });\\n});\\n```\\n\\n### Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.\n\n**Summary:** You're asking whether it's safe to use the individual Azure Monitor exporter extension methods (`AddAzureMonitorTraceExporter`, `AddAzureMonitorMetricExporter`, `AddAzureMonitorLogExporter`) directly instead of the convenience method `UseAzureMonitorExporter`, while keeping your existing OTel configuration (custom samplers, selective instrumentation, etc.).\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Core question:** Does bypassing `UseAzureMonitorExporter` risk missing important SDK-level behaviors such as duplicate data, missing Azure SDK HTTP instrumentation, or other side effects?\n\n**What `UseAzureMonitorExporter` typically does beyond wiring exporters:**\n- Registers Azure SDK Activity source instrumentation automatically\n- Configures live metrics sampling/enrichment\n- May set up enrichers or processors specific to Azure Monitor\n\n**Considerations:**\n- Using the individual `AddAzureMonitor*Exporter` methods should be safe for export, but you may need to manually add `AddAzureClientActivity()` (or equivalent) to capture telemetry from Azure SDK calls\n- Custom samplers set via `SetSampler` work independently of exporters — this should be fine\n- The `EnableLiveMetrics` / `DisableOfflineStorage` options are per-exporter config and should work the same way\n\n**Suggested investigation:**\n- Review the source of [`UseAzureMonitorExporter`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore) to see what additional registrations it performs beyond adding exporters\n- Look for any `AddAzureMonitorActivitySource` or similar calls in that method\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Label Selection\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — this is a question about SDK behavior and usage patterns\n- **Service:** `Monitor -
